summary: Story is ready for implementation with scope-risk notes. The prior NO-GO blockers have been addressed by concrete task expansion, repo-aligned file guidance, security requirements, and specific test expectations.

notes: Required sections from story-tmpl.yaml are present: Status, Executor Assignment, Story, Acceptance Criteria, CodeRabbit Integration, Tasks / Subtasks, Dev Notes, Change Log, Dev Agent Record, QA Results.
No unfilled template variables remain in PO/SM-owned implementation sections.
TBD placeholders are confined to future Dev/QA-owned sections.
executor is @dev and quality_gate is @architect; they are different and recognized agents.
Scope spans frontend, API/proxy, typed contracts, security, agents, and integration work. This is acceptable as a completion story but increases coordination risk.

should_fix_issues.recommendation: If implementation stalls, split into visual/data, pipeline UI, API/handoff, agents/audit, and security-test child stories.

should_fix_issues.summary: Environment variables and backend proxy configuration are implied but not enumerated.
should_fix_issues.recommendation: During implementation, update root or portal .env.example for any Click proxy/backend URLs or server-only credentials introduced.

should_fix_issues.summary: Quality gate owner is @architect while the story includes security and API behavior.
should_fix_issues.recommendation: Architect gate should explicitly review security boundaries, API/proxy contracts, and cross-module integration behavior.

passes: Story status is Draft and valid for PO validation.
Acceptance criteria are concrete and covered by task groups.
Tasks map to ACs and include implementation sequence across source surface, typed contracts, UI, API/handoff, security, and tests.
Dev Notes summarize product, architecture, data model, API, component, file location, and testing context.
Core source references exist and were spot-checked: TASKS_CLICK.md, SPEC_TASKLIST.md, PRD.md, DOCUMENTO_GERAL.md, and architecture fallback docs.
File structure guidance is aligned to the actual repo surface under apps/portal and packages/*.
Security requirements include X-User-Id propagation, no direct FastAPI client calls, workspace/user isolation, append-only audit/history, and Admin-only agent controls.
Testing guidance covers unit, component, API/proxy, and security scenarios.
CodeRabbit disabled skip notice is present; core-config.yaml has no enabled coderabbit_integration setting, so manual review fallback applies.
Code intelligence duplicate check found no duplicate functionality.

notes: Technical claims are traceable to product or architecture documents.
The story explicitly resolves the mismatch between product reference paths and this repo's apps/portal implementation surface.
No invented libraries or unsupported framework choices were identified.
Validation skipped because CodeRabbit is not enabled in core-config.yaml.
Story contains the required disabled notice and does not include CodeRabbit task checkboxes.
